commit.message:
Fix CD pipeline: wait for CI on version bump PR (#255)

## Summary
- CD has been unable to publish for ~11 hours due to a deadlock in the
version bump step
- `gh pr merge --admin` cannot bypass the required `build` status check
on newer GitHub rulesets
- Rush's bump commits contain `[skip ci]` which prevents CI from ever
running on the bump PR

## Fix
- Push an empty commit (without `[skip ci]`) to the bump branch to
trigger CI
- Wait for the `build` check to pass (10min timeout) before merging the
PR
- Removed `[skip ci]` from the PR title to avoid any skip behavior

## Version Reset
- Reset all lockstep package versions from 0.14.0 back to 0.13.0 (last
published to npm)
- Removed changelog entries for unpublished versions 0.13.1–0.14.0
- Created a single minor change file to represent all the unpublished
changes
- Cleaned up 3 stale `version/bump-*` branches from previous failed CD
runs

## Test plan
- [ ] Merge this PR and verify the next CD run with a real version bump
succeeds
- [ ] The existing change files include a `minor` bump, so the next
merge to main should trigger an actual publish

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix cloud_agent merge backend: comment on PR instead of creating separate issue (#597)

* Initial plan

* fix: cloud_agent merge backend now comments on PR instead of creating new issue

The cloud agent backend for merge conflict resolution was creating a new
GitHub issue and assigning the Copilot coding agent to it. However, the
agent works on its own branch and cannot perform a proper git merge across
branches, resulting in PRs with 0 actual changes.

This changes the cloud_agent backend to post a @copilot comment directly
on the conflicting PR, asking the agent to reapply the changes on top of
the latest base branch. This approach works because the agent operates
within the PR context rather than on a separate branch.

Changes:
- core/src/state.rs: Add `comment_only` field to PendingMergeIssue
- cli/src/merge.rs: resolve_via_cloud_agent now uses comment_on_pr
- cli/src/merge.rs: promote_pending_merge_issues handles comment-only
  entries by checking PR conflict status
- Updated and added tests for the new behavior

Co-authored-by: randymarsh77 <1606704+randymarsh77@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address code review: fix unwrap_or for mergeable check, improve docs

commit.message:
[bug] Add pre-flight API connectivity check before Flux installation (#2992)

* Initial plan

* feat: add pre-flight in-cluster API connectivity check before GitOps installation

Add WaitForInClusterAPIConnectivity in pkg/k8s/readiness/connectivity.go
that creates a short-lived busybox pod to verify TCP connectivity to the
API server ClusterIP from within the cluster. This catches eBPF dataplane
race conditions where Cilium DaemonSet pods report Ready but pod-to-service
routing is not yet fully programmed.

Integrate the check into waitForClusterStability in pkg/cli/setup/post_cni.go
as a third step after API server stability and DaemonSet readiness checks.

Closes #3021

commit.message:
feat(cluster): add --ttl flag for ephemeral clusters (#2959)

* feat(cluster): add --ttl flag for ephemeral clusters

Add a --ttl flag to 'ksail cluster create' that auto-destroys clusters
after a configurable duration (e.g. 1h, 30m, 2h30m). The TTL expiry
time is persisted to ~/.ksail/clusters/<name>/ttl.json and displayed in
'ksail cluster list' output when set. State is cleaned up on delete.

- pkg/svc/state/ttl_state.go: TTLInfo struct with SaveClusterTTL,
  LoadClusterTTL helpers; reuses shared constants from cluster_state.go
- pkg/svc/state/ttl_state_test.go: 9 unit tests (round-trip, overwrite,
  IsExpired/Remaining, invalid name validation)
- pkg/cli/cmd/cluster/create.go: --ttl flag + maybeSetClusterTTL saves
  TTL state after successful cluster creation
- pkg/cli/cmd/cluster/delete.go: calls state.DeleteClusterState to clean
  up spec.json + ttl.json after successful deletion
- pkg/cli/cmd/cluster/list.go: loads TTL per cluster and displays
  '[TTL: 1h 23m]' or '[TTL: EXPIRED]' annotations in list output

Closes #2958

Fix DownloadToFileAsync partial-file bug and improve presign error reporting

- DownloadToFileAsync: obtain presigned URL before creating the destination
  file so a presign failure never creates an empty file. On any subsequent
  transfer failure or cancellation, delete the partial file via a finally
  block so callers never see a misleading partial/empty destination.
  Both overloads (basic and access-mode) updated consistently.

- IntegratedS3Client.PresignObjectAsync: replace EnsureSuccessStatusCode()
  with a private EnsurePresignSuccessAsync helper that reads the response
  body before throwing, so the HttpRequestException message contains
  actionable server error detail (e.g. ProblemDetails JSON).

- Tests: added IntegratedS3ClientTests.cs covering PresignObjectAsync
  error-reporting (403, 400, 503, empty body). Added four targeted tests
  in IntegratedS3ClientTransferTests: presign-failure does not create
  file, transfer-error deletes partial file, cancellation deletes partial
  file, and access-mode overload transfer-error deletes partial file.
  Extracted NoOpHttpMessageHandler and added FixedStatusHttpMessageHandler
  as class-level helpers.

commit.message:
Add functionality to enable star tracker constraints to be included in the scheduler (#27)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* Add pre and post sequence overhead parameters to integration calculations (VDA/NIRDA)

* feat: roll-aware visibility with star-tracker earth-limb keep-outs

- Add solar power fraction helpers and roll sweep functions to roll.py
- ScheduleProcessor sweeps candidate rolls (0-360° in roll_step increments)
  filtered by min_power_frac, selecting the roll that maximises visible time
- Roll sweep only activates when st_* constraints are explicitly set;
  vanilla ScheduleProcessor(tle1, tle2) behaves as before
- Fix atomic extend/shrink in _fix_visibility to prevent overlaps when
  remaining duration < min_sequence_duration
- Thread precomputed rolls through _fix_visibility and validate_visibility
- Update pyproject.toml deps to match pandoravisibility (notebook ^7.5.4,
  black ^26.1.0)
- Add test_gap_filling.py (18 tests) and roll sweep tests in test_roll.py
- Add roll-aware-visibility-example.ipynb demo notebook

* docs: fix solar power fraction docstrings — Y is panel hinge axis, not normal

The formula sin(arccos(|dot(Y,sun)|)) is correct and matches
pandoravisibility's get_visibility_best_roll exactly. The docstring
incorrectly stated +Y was the 'solar-panel normal' with Lambert's
cosine law. On Pandora, +Y is the panel hinge axis: power = 1.0
when Y ⊥ Sun (panels face-on), power = 0.0 when Y ∥ Sun (edge-on).

* Update solar panel power fraction threshold and add integration tests for VDA and NIRDA sequences

- Changed the minimum acceptable solar-panel power fraction from 0.8 to 0.7 in the ScheduleProcessor class.
- Added a new integration test file `test_integration_counts.py` to validate the functionality of VDA and NIRDA integration calculations.
- Implemented multiple test cases to ensure correct handling of overheads and frame/integration counts for both VDA and NIRDA sequences.

* fix: correct precomputed_rolls type hints to Dict[str, Optional[float]]

* fix: remove jupyter-contrib-nbextensions (incompatible with notebook v7, breaks mkdocs build)

* Apply suggestion from @Copilot

Breaking up a comment into two lines to meet line length requirements (Black)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* changing min_sequenc_duration

* bumping version

* perf: precompute Sun vectors once per times array in roll sweep

compute_mean_solar_power was called inside the candidate-roll loop
and iterated over time samples calling get_sun() per sample, giving
O(N_candidates x N_times) ephemeris lookups per target.

Fix:
- Add _sun_vectors(times) which calls get_sun() once for the whole
  times array and returns an (N, 3) float64 array via vectorised
  numpy ops (no Python loop).
- Add _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs(ra, dec, roll_deg, sun_vecs)
  which computes the mean power fraction from a precomputed sun_vecs
  array using a single batched dot-product (sun_vecs @ y_payload).
- Refactor compute_mean_solar_power to delegate to both helpers,
  removing the per-sample Python loop (public API unchanged).
- find_best_roll_for_target now precomputes sun_vecs = _sun_vectors(times)
  once before the candidate loop and calls _mean_solar_power_from_sun_vecs
  inside, reducing ephemeris cost from O(N_candidates x N_times) to
  O(N_times).

103 tests pass, black/flake8 clean.

* changing default post sequence to 120 s

commit.message:
fix(ha-addon): AppArmor enforce mode — rix for scripts, signal rules

Key fixes:
- ix → rix for /init, /s6-init, /bin/**, /usr/bin/**, /package/**,
  /command/** — shell scripts need read for shebang parsing
- Added signal (send) rule for S6 process management
- Added /dev/tty rw, /run/{,**} rwk, /etc/cont-finish.d/**
- /app/** rixm — allow memory-mapped Python .pyc files
- /tmp/** rwk — lock files need k (lock)
- Removed complain flag — profile is now in enforce mode

  inference; revert to simple name [active] (created_at) display
- Update terminal.lua cmd_name() to read CliTool[1].name after normalization
- Add <leader>at and <C-a>t default keymaps for select_tool()
- Update README.md and doc/aiwaku.nvim.txt with multi-tool cmd format
  and select_tool() API docs

commit.message:
fix: add /s6-init wrapper to fix /init permission denied on restart

Docker overlayfs can strip execute permissions from /init on
container restart. The /s6-init wrapper re-applies chmod +x
before exec-ing /init.
